title: Srimad Bhagavatam | HG Chanchalapathi Dasa | SB 3.28.7 | 03-09-2016
speaker: HG Chanchalapathi Dasa

segments.text: Satsang with Mooji
Aetair Anyais Cha
The general yoga process entails observing the rules and regulations,
practicing the different sitting postures,
concentrating the mind on the vital circulation of the air,
and then thinking of the supreme personality,
of Godhead, in his Vaikuntha pastimes.
This is the general process of yoga.
This same concentration can be achieved by other recommended processes,
and therefore Anyais Cha, other methods, also can be applied.
The essential point is that
the mind which is contaminated by material attractions
has to be bridled and concentrated on the supreme personality of Godhead.
It cannot be fixed on something void or impersonal.
For this reason,
so-called yoga practices of voidism and impersonalism
are not recommended in any standard yoga shastra.
The real yogi is the devotee
because his mind is always concentrated
on the pastimes of Lord Krishna.
Therefore, Krishna consciousness is the topmost yoga system.
So, we are reading Kapila's instructions to his mother
on the subject of yoga
and the different steps and stages of yoga practice.
And in the last verse, we saw
that how one must concentrate on the pastimes of Lord Krishna.
How one must control the vital air,
the circulation of the vital air,
and finally become absorbed in Vaikuntha Leela.
Continuing that, here Kapila is saying,
etair Anyais Cha Pathibhir.
This is what is to be achieved.
To control the mind
and enable the mind to concentrate,
on the pastimes of Vishnu
in Vaikuntha.
Anyais Cha Pathibhir.
Etair Anyais Cha Pathibhir.
So this can also be achieved by other processes.
To control the mind
and concentrate the mind
on Vishnu and Vishnu's pastimes
can also be done
by other processes, anyapathah, etair anyaishchapatibhir, mano dushtam asatpatham.
But the important thing is this mind which is dushta, which is contaminated, unbridled.
That mind should be brought away from asatpatham, asat, temporary, patham, the path, and Prabhupada has translated that as on the path of material enjoyment.
So the mind has a nature, has a tendency to go into the asatpatham.
Because mano dushtam, and so this has to be controlled.
How do we control? Budhya yunjitha, by intelligence we must control the mind.
Intelligence is higher than the mind. Mind is a repository of thoughts, feelings, emotions, desires.
So mind comes up with all of these desires.
And thoughts and it
is the budhya yunjitha.
Buddhi has to control.
And it is the intelligence which tells us, this is okay, this is not okay, do this, don't do this.
So we very clearly see the distinction between the role of the mind, and the role of the intelligence.
mind comes up with that.
And what mental alémṭham?

and it is the intelligence which will tell no no we must control we will have health problem if i

so if i eat this again that suffering will come it's the intelligence which is functioning
the two are distinct so buddhi should be used to bridle the mind
and it won't happen immediately gradually slowly little by little
because for a long time we have left the mind uncontrolled because controlling the mind

which is always attracted by material enjoyment asatpatham
and thus fix himself in the thought of the supreme personality of godhead
and pravapāda has mentioned that
Clearly Kapila is saying this controlling the mind with the intelligence and being alert that is possible by Ashtanga Yoga and it may be possible by other methods also.
But the important thing is to control the mind with the intelligence and that also requires the intelligence to be always very alert.
Srila Prabhupada also mentions, there is this verse from the Upanishads which talks about how spiritual life is like a
kshurasya dhara nishitha duratyaya durgam patastat kava yo vadanti
In this verse, it is described the spiritual life is like a sharp
razor's edge and just like when you are shaving, you have to be very alert.
If you are not alert and you have a very sharp razor, you may cut your face.
So what is needed when you are having a sharp razor, you have to be very alert.
So the same principle atandritaha.
As spiritual life is like a sharp razor, you have to be very alert.
But for those spiritualists who are trying to practice Krishna consciousness, we have to be alert.
Atandritaha also means not lazy, not tired.
So the intelligence has to be very alert.
So we should lead a life in such a way that our mind and intelligence can be properly
engaged in spiritual life and especially the intelligence is expected to be very
alert watching over the thoughts always discerning on the basis of intelligence
here buddhyayun jita buddhi has to control now the buddhi has is one's own intelligence
another aspect of intelligence is intelligence coming from the scriptures because our
intelligence is also limited we cannot understand many things about this complex material world so
we need direction from the Vedic literatures
so on the basis of one's own intelligence and also on the basis of intelligence developed
from Vedic knowledge one should control the mind and one should always be alert there is
another example given in the bhagavatam when a hunter hunts an animal it is said that after
capturing the animal he will always be very alert
about the animal because the animal can run away again so in the same way one is to control the
mind and also to be always alert otherwise the mind will again asat patham mano dushyam asat patham
mind will once again tread on the path of asat patham
so
another example pravpaadt gives is
if you are walking in a forest
under a lot of thorns
imagine you are bare footed
and you are walking
how careful you have to be
where you place
your every step
you have to be alert
in the same way we have to
be wage
we have to be alert
every step
or every stage
of our thinking and acting process. So in other words alertness is very important
and in the same context Prabhupada also informs us that we should never trust
the mind because the mind is dushtha mana. Mano dushtham asat patham. So we have to be alert
and never trust the mind, never feel overconfident about the mind because mind is dushtha. So as
practicing devotees we always seek guidance. Guidance means intelligence. Either we have
developed a certain amount of spiritual intelligence and we seek that guidance or
we seek guidance from the scriptures.
From the spiritual master's instructions or we can also take guidance from a more experienced
devotee in the form of association. All of these helps to be on the right path of Krishna
consciousness rather than trust the mind and go by the mind's thinking and judgment. So this is an
important principle.
The next important thing in this verse and in the purport that Prabhupada mentions
Mano dushtham asat patham. The essential point is that the mind which is contaminated by material
attraction, mind is contaminated by material attraction, has to be bridled and controlled
has to be bridled and concentrated on the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So let us think a little bit on this
principle of material attraction. Our minds are attracted to material enjoyment and here Prabhupada
explains that is the symptom of a contaminated mind.
Mano dushtham asat patham. The essential point in this verse and in the purport that Prabhupada explains that is the symptom of a contaminated mind.
The essential point in this verse and in the purport that Prabhupada explains that is the symptom of a contaminated mind.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
A contaminated mind is attracted to sense gratification. Now this attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention.
Now, this attraction. Attraction means our consciousness, something it grabs our attention, something it holds our attention, something that evokes our interest, our liking.

And that's why we get attracted to something. Just like all of us have this experience. We go out in the auto or we go out in the car. When you're driving, you're looking at things outside. Immediately there are many, many, many things which attract our attention.
Right? Those are the objects of senses. Indriya Vishaya.
It attracts us.
It attracts our attention.
And this attention is a feature of consciousness.
This table has no attention.
It has no consciousness.
So moment we say there is a soul, and the soul has consciousness, and the soul has the ability to be attracted to different thing.
The table is not attracted to anything.
If there is an ant crawling on the floor, the ant is attracted to something and hence it is walking and crawling and heading somewhere because it is attracted.
So this principle of being attracted itself is a feature of our consciousness.
Now the mayavadi's approach is, this attraction is a wrong thing.
The Vaishnava's philosophy is, no, no, no.
Attraction itself is not a wrong thing. Being attracted to Indriya Vishaya is a wrong thing.
The feature of being attracted is not a wrong thing.
In fact, it is the feature of being attracted.
We like it. We are drawn to it.
We are charmed by it.
And that's what gives us pleasure.
So the feature of being attracted is not wrong.
That's a feature of the consciousness.
Table has no attraction. Every living entity will always have attraction.
Now we have to decide where we are, to what we are attracted.
When it is mano dushtam, asat patham.
When the mind is not dushtam, it is shuddham, then it is sat patham.
The mind can be attracted to the right thing.
So this is an important understanding.
When the mind is contaminated, we are attracted to sense gratification and other objects of senses in the material world.
But the principle of attraction is a very fundamental feature of consciousness.
That cannot be negated.
In fact, Shila Prabhupada brings out this in a very interesting way.
In a letter that Prabhupada wrote.
He says, actually Prabhupada could express very profound Krishna conscious ideas so spontaneously, so easily.
Because he was always on that spiritual platform.
He was fully conscious of all of these knowledge.
So even when he is writing a letter, this letter was being written to an American.
He was not even a devotee. Later on he became a devotee.
And in that letter Prabhupada writes,
The sum and substance of material life is attraction between man and woman.
The sum and substance of spiritual life is attraction between Krishna and living entity.
It's a very profound thought.
Material life means there is attraction between man and woman.
And it is actually this for the materialists, for the conditioned souls, for the ignorant living entities.
It is this attraction which actually gives them pleasure and enjoyment.
Attraction between man and woman.
Now, the next thing Prabhupada is...
And Prabhupada is saying this.
The sum and substance of material life is this.
Everything is centered around this.
Attraction between a man and a woman.
Now, this attraction is actually very...
Is what makes life interesting.
So, what is spiritual life going to be?
No, no, no, don't think there is no attraction in the spiritual world.
Spiritual life also full of attraction.
Because it's the attractive things which make life interesting.
In the spiritual world, spiritual life is attraction between Krishna and the living entity.
It is such a great relief.
The Mayavadis say...
Material life...
Mayavadis also say the first half of the sentence.
They say sum and substance of material life is attraction between man and woman.
But when it comes to spiritual life, they don't know that variegatedness.
And they say you should be free from all attraction.
All attraction for no attraction at all.
Means you become a stone.
This is the kind of an understanding that the Mayavadis have.
And the Vaishnavas say no, no, no.
That's not the truth.
Spiritual life also full of attraction.
And attractive features are there in the spiritual world.
And that attractive feature is between the Lord, the living entity and the Lord in the spiritual world.
There are wonderful pastimes.
There are wonderful situations created by Yogamaya in the spiritual world.
And the living entity is a participant in all of those activities which are extremely attractive.
That is the view of spiritual world and spiritual consciousness presented in the Vedic literature.
A very nice verse which tells us about this is...
Kunti's prayers.
We have all chanted this verse.
You are all familiar with this verse.
Kunti Devi is saying...
Matir Madhupate Sakrit.
May Matihi, my consciousness, my mind, may it be always be drawn to Madhupate.
Madhupati who is Krishna.
Ananya Vishaya and no other thought, no other subject but only Krishna.
And how should it be?
Rati Mudvahata Daddha.
Rati means attraction.
So I should be drawn.
I should be attracted to Krishna.
How I should be attracted to Krishna?
With the force.
Like Ganga is flowing towards the ocean.
If you go to Haridwar, any of you must have seen.
And when you see the Ganga flowing there.
One thing you can notice is the force with which Ganga is flowing.
There is some kind of a force.
There is some kind of a something drawing Ganga to run, rushing towards the ocean.
Kunti is praying, may my consciousness also rush towards Krishna.
You see that element of attraction.
When you are attracted to something, it is irresistible.
You are drawn to it.
So Kunti is praying that may my consciousness be also attracted like that.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's teaching says,
In spiritual life, initially we go through a phase where we follow rules and regulations,
controlling the mind, controlling the senses, attentive, be very alert.
That's a very essential phase.
But that's not the end.
The end of all spiritual life is not just that.
When we are purified by doing this,
we will come to a stage that Kunti Maharani is talking about.
Ratimuddhva dada dha.
When one will be drawn to Krishna.
And that stage is called Raganuga Bhakti.
These are very important contributions of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
It is there in the Vedic literatures.
Prior to that, many Acharyas in many other Sampradayas,
this understanding is not highlighted.
It is there, but it is not highlighted.
But these are special features.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu highlighted these concepts, these principles from the Shastras.
To give you an illustration of what this is,
I have mentioned this story.
There was one, there is one supporter of our temple.
One day he called and he said,
Swamiji, we have built a new house.
We want you to come and do a puja in my house.
So I said, okay, he is an important man.
We will send some, our devotees go and do Narsimha Yagna or something.
He said, okay.
And then he asked, Swamiji, how long will that puja be?
So I said, maybe one hour, one hour fifteen minutes.
He said, no, no, no, no, Swamiji, not that long.
I said, okay.
So shall we make it forty-five minutes?
No, no, no, no, even forty-five minutes is too long.
Then I could hear his wife on the background shouting at him.
No, no, no, don't say all that to Swamiji, etc.
And finally he said, fifteen minutes.
Just see, they have no attraction for these things.
They want some puja.
They want some puja you have to do.
We have opened a new house.
Somehow finish it off, finish quickly.
Because they have no attraction.
For them, it is some kind of a vidhi.
We have to do what to do.
My wife is pushing me to do.
For her satisfaction, we have to do one puja.
You see, so there is no attraction in that.
The very same person, another day, I had to call him for something.
And then I called him.
For a long time he didn't lift the phone.
Usually he takes the phone very quickly.
He is always on the phone.
So I was wondering.
And then I stopped.
And again after a few minutes, again I called.
Again after a long time, he picked up the phone.
And I could hear on the other side, there was a lot of excitement in their house.
I think he was in the house.
And I could sense a kind of...
And then he is telling,
Swamiji, if you don't mind.
If you don't mind.
No, we are watching India-Pakistan cricket match.
If you don't mind, I will call you after 15 minutes.
You see.
So when it comes to a cricket match,
maybe something interesting must be happening.
You know, we are all disconnected from that world.
We don't know.
And so when it comes to a cricket match,
they are drawn to it.
They are attracted to it.
So, and they think that spiritual life means
all these boring process, pratha and all these puja, vidhi, all that you have to do.
How can we do it in a very short while?
Cricket match.
Oh, so interesting.
One hour.
Two hours.
Five days.
All that they are ready to endure.
You see, because there the element of attraction is working.
And they, because they are not spiritually trained,
they don't know about these things.
They think that spiritual life or religious life, religious activities
are all simply some kind of a boring rituals that one has to follow.
Because it's in the tradition
or the wife is troubling.
And that's the only impetus for them.
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says,
no, no, no, spiritual life is not just that.
That's an essential stage.
It's a stage that is essential for purification.
Then you'll come to the stage of raga-anuga bhakti.
Actually, the word raga-anuga,
anuga, ga means gamishyati, to go.
Anuga means to follow.
And follow what?
Raga-anuga.
Raga means attraction.
So you will come to a stage where you will be drawn to Krishna
and you will be going in that direction.
That stage is called raga-anuga bhakti.
Rati muddhva hata adha-gange vaugam udhanvati.
It is not that Ganga is flowing down the river like that
with great ferocity like that
because they have put one big pump
and the pump is pushing the water.
No, no, no, no.
No pump is required in Ganga
when Ganga is flowing.
Ganga is flowing spontaneously
with such velocity.
In the same way,
we can come to a stage
where our consciousness will be drawn to Krishna.
Like that.
And not attracted to anything else.

newer and newer rasas

if I just think about it, mukhavikaraha

very very attractive
spiritual world
